title: prunus × gondouinii
intro: Prunus × gondouinii, also known as apricot kernel, is a plant species that is a hybrid of Prunus armeniaca (apricot) and Prunus persica (peach). The kernel of the apricot seed has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of amygdalin and other compounds. Prunus × gondouinii has been used to support overall health and wellness, with potential benefits including anti-cancer and anti-oxidant properties, as well as support for digestive and immune system function. Apricot kernel is commonly prepared as a tincture or oil, and is also available in capsule or powder form, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine practices.
